title: GenSwab Assay (SARS-CoV-2)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f24dh","text":"The GenapSys SARS-CoV-2 GenSwab Assay is a reverse transcription sequencing (RT-Seq) test. The assay uses primers and probes that were first designed by OctantBio to detect RNA from SARS-CoV-2 in respiratory and saliva specimens from patients. This is the workflow for purified RNA samples, and starts with purified samples. Our protocol will accept RNA purified from any kit.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dvfgq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Start with a stock solution of 1% Tween-20 in TE. Dilute 10x in TE to obtain 0.1% Tween-20 in TE.

Retrieve S2 spike-in RNA from -80C freezer. S2 spike-in RNA is provided at 1e10 copies/ul. Perform three 100x dilutions, followed by a 10x dilution to obtain 1000 copies/ul. Dilutions should be done in Lo-Bind DNA tubes in 0.1% Tween-20 in TE.
Add 198ul to 0.1% Tween-20 in TE to the tube containing the S2 spike-in RNA. This is the Dilution 1.
Remove 2ul of Dilution 1 to 198ul of 0.1% Tween-20 in TE. This is Dilution 2.
Remove 2ul of Dilution 2 to 198ul of 0.1% Tween-20 in TE. This is Dilution 3.
Make a final 10x dilution by adding 10ul of Dilution 3 to 90ul of 0.1% Tween-20 in TE. Keep on ice until use.
Each batch of reactions should be accompanied by 1 positive control and 1 no-template control, with a minimum of 1 set of controls per 96-well plate.

While the primer and sample mix incubates on ice, prepare the mastermix. The mastermix comprises the reagents in blue. Combine the TaqPath Master Mix, diluted S2 spike-in RNA and water in the necessary quantities, and mix well by pipetting.
To the 2 ul of 10x Indexed Primer Mix in each well, add 5 ul of the sample (purified RNA).
Dispense 13 ul of MasterMix into each well.
Seal the plate. Spin down the plate at 1000xg for 1 min to collect the contents, then mix using a plate mixer at 2000 rpm for 2 min (96-well plate). Load into thermocycler.
Run the following PCR program:
Pool the samples. Remove 5ul from each reaction to be combined to a fresh tube or sterile reservoir (if using a multi-channel pipettor).
Transfer the reaction mix from the reservoir to a tube of appropriate size. Vortex to mix. Transfer 100ul to a fresh Lo-Bind 1.5ml tube.
Clean up using a dual-sided purification. Add 50 µL of AmpureXP beads. Vortex to mix, and incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Use a magnetic rack to collect the beads for at least 1 minute.
Transfer supernatant to a new tube. Add 130 µL of AmpureXP beads. Vortex and incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Use magnetic rack to collect the beads for at least 1 minute.
Remove and discard supernatant.
Wash the beads twice with 500 µL of freshly made 80% ethanol.
After the final wash, leave the lid open for 3 minutes to let the residual ethanol evaporate.
Elute in 40 µL of TE buffer. Vortex to mix and incubate for 2 minutes. Spin down briefly to collect.
Use magnetic rack to collect the beads for at least 1 minute. Collect 35ul of the eluate.
Dilute the eluted library 1:10 in TE buffer: add 5ul of library to 45ul TE buffer.
Quantify the 1:10 dilution using the High Sensitivity DNA Qubit kit.
Convert the Qubit measurement to molarity assuming a 120bp amplicon. For convenience, an online tool like NEB's NEBioCalculator can provide the conversion.

Optional: qPCR measurement of the library would provide a more accurate concentration, and is recommended for initial tests. Subsequent tests can extrapolate from the conversion between Qubit and qPCR measurements.
Dilute the eluted library with TE to 33pM in Lo-bind tubes.
Proceed to clonal amplification. The suggested starting point for clonal amplification is 4ul of the 33pM library.
Incubate the primer and sample mix at 65 °C for 5 min, then place on ice for at least 2 min.
creator: huiyi.chen
